methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 1 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 2 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 7 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Nano Nuclear Energy

data.narrative:
Oklo is a public-stage nuclear energy company founded by Jacob DeWitte and Caroline Cochran, currently valued at $10B+ following $500M+ in total capital raised. The valuation analysis draws from a nuclear energy sector universe comprising 3 public peers (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and 8 private competitors including Nano Nuclear Energy, TerraPower, X-Energy, Kairos Power, and Zap Energy, for a total of 11 comparable entities.

Peer-median triangulation was not applicable to this analysis. The nuclear energy sector contains only seven same-sector peers overall, with insufficient overlap when filtered by stage, adjacent-stage status, or valuation tier—the peer-median method requires a minimum of three comparable datapoints within these narrow criteria. No triangulated valuation output was produced, as applying an all-sector median would introduce material methodological bias given the specialized nature of nuclear technology and the thin comparable set at Oklo's public-company scale.

The sparse, heterogeneous peer universe—dominated by early-stage private developers alongside a handful of public entities at divergent scales—presents a structural constraint on market-based valuation synthesis. Key uncertainties include the maturity and capital requirements of individual peer pipelines, regulatory pathway clarity across competitors, and the degree to which public market pricing for NNE and SMR reflects investor sentiment toward the broader advanced nuclear sector versus company-specific fundamentals. Additional transaction data or revenue/cash-flow baselines would be required to support a defensible valuation range.
